intro: AppMetrica exposes a free tier while Airbridge starts at $199/month. AppMetrica is positioned for indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution, while Airbridge leans toward mobile. In practice, Yandexs free real-time mobile app analytics and install attribution platform with crash reporting and push notifications. Airbridge — Mobile attribution and incrementality platform with deep linking, web-to-app tracking, and SKAdNetwork support. AppMetrica carries medium lock-in (Yandex analytics, data export limited) while Airbridge is low (MMP attribution data in proprietary schema). AppMetrica rates 5/5 on transparency versus 3/5 for Airbridge. Listed strengths unique to AppMetrica include free with unlimited events and real-time reports, while Airbridge lists mobile apps with paid acquisition on its side. Reach for AppMetrica when you need free mobile attribution and analytics for apps with yandex ad traffic; reach for Airbridge when the job is mobile apps with paid acquisition. The honest trade-off: AppMetrica is not built for eu or us apps with strict vendor, sanctions or data-residency policies, and Airbridge is not the right call for web-only products, so neither is a universal default.
quick_take: AppMetrica is for indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution; Airbridge is for mobile; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose AppMetrica if indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution and you need a free tier to validate fit.
verdict_b: Choose Airbridge if mobile and the $199/month entry price fits your budget.

faq.a: AppMetrica has a free tier and Airbridge starts at $199/month, so at the lowest end AppMetrica is effectively cheaper. Once you exceed the free limits, the comparison depends on per-unit rates not published here. Price against your actual send or usage volume.

faq.q: Can I migrate from AppMetrica to Airbridge?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving AppMetrica is moderate effort at medium lock-in (Yandex analytics, data export limited), and onboarding to Airbridge should be straightforward given its low lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, AppMetrica or Airbridge?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, AppMetrica rates 4/5 while Airbridge rates 3/5, giving AppMetrica the edge. Scores compress a lot of variance though — SDK language coverage, error messages, and docs quality matter more than the number. Run a quick integration spike on both before trusting the gap.

faq.q: Is Airbridge a good alternative to AppMetrica?
faq.a: Airbridge is an alternative in the same category, but its stated sweet spot is mobile, while AppMetrica's is indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution. If your job matches AppMetrica's profile more closely, switching to Airbridge is a compromise. Use the best-fit match, not brand familiarity, to decide. Note: Airbridge is explicitly not for web-only products.
